title: onosma cinerea subsp. sanguinolenta
intro: Onosma cinerea subsp. sanguinolenta, also known as blood root or red root, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Asia. The roots of this plant have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Onosma cinerea subsp. sanguinolenta include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Onosma cinerea subsp. sanguinolenta can be found in the form of dried root powder or liquid extracts, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
